title: AI-generated content identified in external communications
trigger: Manual report or DLP detection of AI-generated content in outbound emails, reports, or client deliverables. Triggered by DET-MS-PV-001 content-inspection policies flagging AI-characteristic patterns, or DET-MS-SEN-005 detecting unusual volumes of content with AI markers.

immediate.action: Identify the scope of the AI-generated content: which documents, emails, or deliverables are affected. Determine whether the content has been sent externally or is still in draft.
Assess whether the content is client-facing, regulatory-facing, or has potential legal or reputational impact. If regulatory filing: escalate to Legal immediately.
Preserve evidence: capture the content metadata, AI-generation indicators, and distribution list. Do not alter or recall the content without Legal guidance.

short_term.action: Review the AI-generated content for accuracy, hallucinations, and factual errors. Document any inaccuracies found.

Determine disclosure obligations. Consider: industry-specific AI disclosure requirements, contractual obligations with clients, and regulatory guidance on AI-generated content in the applicable jurisdiction.
If disclosure is required: draft disclosure communication in coordination with Legal. If not required: document the determination and rationale.

long_term.action: Develop or update the organization's AI content disclosure policy. Define which content types require human review, disclosure, or prohibition of AI generation.
Implement a content review workflow for high-risk content types (regulatory filings, client deliverables, press releases). Consider AI-content detection tooling in the review pipeline.
Issue all-employee education communication (comms/all-employee-education) reinforcing expectations around AI-generated content and disclosure requirements.

decision_tree.node: Has the AI-generated content been sent externally?
decision_tree.yes: Assess regulatory and client impact immediately.

decision_tree.node: Does the content contain factual errors or hallucinations?
decision_tree.yes: Initiate correction and consider recall/retraction with Legal guidance.

decision_tree.node: Are there regulatory or contractual AI disclosure requirements?
decision_tree.yes: Draft and issue disclosure per Legal guidance.
decision_tree.no: Document determination; update policy to prevent recurrence.

metrics_to_capture: Number of AI-generated content incidents per quarter
Percentage involving client-facing or regulatory content
Time from detection to content review completion (target: ≤ 5 business days)
Recurrence rate after employee education (target: declining trend)

references.title: NIST AI RMF — MAP-3 AI risks and benefits
EU AI Act — Transparency obligations for AI-generated content
